name: Tucson
tagline: Desert Sunshine, Low Costs, and 350 Days of Blue Sky
description: Tucson is one of the most affordable cities in the American West, offering stunning Sonoran Desert landscapes, a UNESCO-designated food scene, and over 350 days of sunshine per year. Arizona has a flat income tax rate that keeps more money in your pocket, and housing costs are remarkably low compared to Phoenix and other Sun Belt cities. For FIRE retirees who love warm weather and wide-open spaces, Tucson is a hidden gem.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens
quickFacts.safetyRating: Moderate – varies by neighborhood; central and northeast areas are safest
quickFacts.climate: Hot desert with mild winters; summers exceed 100°F but humidity is very low

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Midtown, Sam Hughes, or a casita near the University

dining.description: Sonoran hot dogs, taquerias, and casual dining 2-3x/week

healthcare.description: ACA marketplace Silver plan; Arizona premiums are moderate

transportation.description: Paid-off car with basic insurance; car is essential in Tucson's spread-out layout

entertainment.description: Hiking Sabino Canyon and Saguaro National Park, free desert trails, community events

1M.lifestyle: A one-bedroom casita near Midtown or Sam Hughes, cooking at home most nights supplemented by cheap and excellent taquerias, and a paid-off car. Tucson's low rents make this one of the more comfortable $1M cities in the US. Sabino Canyon and Saguaro National Park provide free outdoor recreation year-round. The main budget watch is summer A/C -- electricity bills spike from June through September -- but the rest of the year is utility-light.

housing.description: 2BR home in the Catalina Foothills or a nice place in Sam Hughes with character

dining.description: Regular dining at Tucson's UNESCO-recognized food scene; upscale Southwestern cuisine

healthcare.description: ACA Gold plan with dental and vision; comfortable coverage

transportation.description: Reliable car, full coverage insurance, gas for desert road trips

entertainment.description: Desert Museum membership, horseback riding, stargazing tours, pool access, gym

2M.lifestyle: A two-bedroom home in the Catalina Foothills or a place with character in Sam Hughes, dining out several times a week at Tucson's UNESCO-recognized food scene, and a reliable car for desert road trips. You can afford a Desert Museum membership, horseback riding, and a good health plan. Weekends might include drives up Mount Lemmon or trips to Kartchner Caverns. The dry heat is real in summer, but A/C and pool access make it manageable.

housing.description: 3BR home with pool in the Catalina Foothills or Oro Valley with mountain views

dining.description: Fine dining at top restaurants, private wine tastings, weekly date nights

healthcare.description: Premium private insurance with comprehensive coverage and wellness programs

transportation.description: Late-model SUV, premium insurance, road trips to Sedona and the Grand Canyon

entertainment.description: Country club or resort membership, golf, spa treatments, art gallery openings

3M.lifestyle: A three-bedroom home with a pool in the Catalina Foothills or Oro Valley, regular fine dining at Tucson's best restaurants, and a late-model SUV. Premium health insurance, a resort or country club membership, and regular road trips to Sedona and the Grand Canyon are all within budget. Tucson's low costs mean $10K a month goes far here -- you live comfortably in a desirable area with real room for travel and hobbies.

housing.description: Luxury desert estate in the Foothills or a custom hacienda in Tanque Verde Valley

dining.description: Private chef, exclusive dining experiences, catered desert dinner parties

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine, executive wellness, comprehensive private insurance

transportation.description: Luxury SUV, second vehicle, occasional flights to Phoenix or LA

entertainment.description: Premium resort memberships, private desert tours, art collecting, Tucson Gem Show VIP

domesticHelp.description: Pool service, weekly house cleaning, landscaping, handyman on call

luxuryExtras.description: Quarterly travel, spa retreats, Western art collecting, ranch experiences

5M.lifestyle: A large desert estate in the Foothills or Tanque Verde Valley with a pool, a housekeeper who comes several times a week, and concierge medical coverage. You dine at the city's best restaurants regularly, drive a nice SUV, and fly business class for longer trips. A resort membership, regular travel, and art collecting from Tucson's galleries are all within budget. Arizona's low taxes let you keep more of each withdrawal.

housing.description: Sprawling desert compound with guest house, or multiple properties including a ranch

dining.description: Full-time personal chef, exclusive wine cellar, catered events and galas

healthcare.description: Top concierge practice, Mayo Clinic access in Scottsdale, full wellness team

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, private car service, chartered flights

entertainment.description: Art patronage, private desert retreats, international travel, museum board involvement

domesticHelp.description: Full-time estate staff including housekeeper, groundskeeper, personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: Ranch property, first-class international travel, art collection, philanthropy, horse stables

10M.lifestyle: A large desert property with guest house and mountain views, a cook who comes in several times a week, full-time domestic help, and top-tier concierge healthcare with access to the Mayo Clinic in Scottsdale. You travel first class, keep multiple vehicles, and dine out without thinking about the bill. Tucson's low cost of living means this budget creates enormous margin -- enough for a second property, regular charitable giving to conservation and arts organizations, and travel throughout the year.
